meta.title: Substrate Atlas — single source-of-truth data file
meta.purpose: Canonical data for the substrate atlas. Both interfaces (Elie Python CLI toy_4179, Lyra substrate_atlas.html) render from THIS file. Establishes the single source-of-truth (coordination v2 target #1).
meta.tier_authority: Grace's canonical tier registry. The 'tier' field here is a SNAPSHOT; Grace's registry is authoritative. When a bank moves, Grace recolors here (or her registry) and both atlases follow.

tier_meaning.FORCED: banked: passes the full 7-item checklist; counts toward X/26
tier_meaning.DERIVED: mechanism forced (zero free params) but not yet banked (e.g. at a gate)
tier_meaning.IDENTIFIED: a form found that matches (<1%), mechanism plausible, derivation open
tier_meaning.FRAME: a structural/physical reading, consistent, not a number
tier_meaning.OPEN: not yet addressed / blocked on a named wall

meta.author: Elie

nu_axis.landmark: Shilov boundary S^4 / Rac scalar singleton

electron.landmark: BF / marginal / self-dual = the SPECTRAL STRIP self-dual axis (Casey: electron deposits on the spectral strip)
electron.geometry: log (2*Delta=d, marginal correlator); deposit locus = the SPECTRAL STRIP (continuum running-scale), self-dual axis at nu=5/2
electron.math: reference unit; BF-log coeff 9/16 = N_c^2/rank^(n_C-1) (pi-FREE: a non-compact strip, no closed volume)
electron.how: self-dual log mode: correlator runs (no fixed scale) -> the natural mass UNIT; deposits on the spectral strip (continuum); pi-free because the strip is non-compact (no compact-sphere volume to carry pi)

muon.geometry: continuum boundary spread (free scalar, box phi=0)
muon.how: free 5d scalar; mass = concentration = density/vol(S^4) over the 6 so(4) 2-planes; spatial-only S^4 (not S^4 x S^1) -> pi^2
muon.gate: FORCED when FK absolute const = 1 (Cal FK-1994) + Grace 7-item checklist

tau.landmark: vertex / trivial rep / identity = GF(128) additive identity 0 = Frobenius FIXED POINT
tau.geometry: discrete bulk cell count (Reed-Solomon GF(128)); the tau sits at the Frobenius fixed point (additive identity 0)
tau.math: m_tau/m_e = 49*71 - sqrt(pi) = 3477.2275 (vs obs 3477.228, resid 2.1e-7). LEADING = finite discrete linearization 49*71 = g^rank(g+2^C2) (side = Frobenius PERIOD g; forbids 127/128; rank=torus rank; depth = bulk g + boundary 2^C2). CORRECTION = sqrt(pi) = Gamma(1/2) = a CURVATURE residue (Casey: 'sqrt(pi) is a curvature'; the part the discrete linearization can't capture, Curvature Principle); origin OPEN (curvature), not yet derived blind. NB the tau leading form alone is 7.5sigma off the measured tau (4205); the correction carries the prediction; data too coarse (6.75e-5) to select sqrt(pi).
tau.how: discrete tiling at the Frobenius fixed point: pi-FREE because discrete; heaviest because a point; SUM form because additive identity; side g = Frobenius period (vertex-case RIGOROUS, 4195); boundary term = emission layer (depth 2^C2)

neutrino.how: masses + mixing from the same D_IV^5 evaluated three ways; mixing = trajectory-mixing between strata

quark.geometry: colored bulk trajectory (no free colored ground state)
quark.math: cross-tier ratios lead (Georgi-Jarlskog down/lep = N_c^2); bulk-color needed
quark.how: colored trajectory; cooling FORCES color into singlets (confinement); masses = colored cell counts, OPEN

photon.math: massless (zero concentration); spin-1 = Rac(x)Rac / Di(x)Di composite (Flato-Fronsdal)
photon.how: LIGHT = the boundary field that stayed SPREAD (uncommitted -> massless); the tiling before commitment

gluon.math: SU(3) from N_c=3 = h^v(SU(3)); the odd Peirce plane
gluon.how: color = the N_c=3 odd off-diagonal Peirce directions; 8 gluons = su(3) basis on the Hardy-space Toeplitz algebra

W_Z.math: rank=2=h^v(SU(2)_L); sin^2(theta_W) runs; m_Z/m_W via cos(theta_W)
W_Z.how: SU(2)_L sits in K; couplings PROJECT from the 3 strata (don't unify); gauge dynamics await #418

higgs.math: VEV via F85 (a_0=(dim SO(4,2))^2=225); m_H OPEN
higgs.how: the Higgs scale = the single dimensionful anchor ell_B; VEV = bulk-boundary coupling that sets where commitment happens

proton.trajectory: deepest STABLE colored ground state (cold survivor)
proton.math: m_p/m_e = 6 pi^5 (T187); uud forced by Pauli+color (spin-1/2 octet)
proton.how: a full COMMITMENT of 3 colored cells into a color-singlet; the cold survivor of the colored sector (parallel to the electron)

neutron.trajectory: first semi-stable excitation; beta-decays to p
neutron.math: m_n/m_p = 1.0014; n -> p + e + nu (trajectory relaxation) + ~0.3% radiative photon
neutron.how: the proton's first excitation; beta-decay = relaxation to the ground state, emitting the trio + small radiation

pion.trajectory: the boundary-emission mode of the colored sector
pion.math: light hadron cells ~ pi^(n_C) (T185/T2488); Goldstone of chiral symmetry
pion.how: a quark-antiquark COMMITMENT CIRCLE emitting at the color-blind boundary (only singlets, F94); the Goldstone = lightest strong-sector boundary emission

mass.how: mass = concentration = density/volume; formal degree is the Plancherel density, divided by the realization volume. point = heaviest, spread = lightest.

spin.how: spin-1/2 = the Di spinor singleton (nu=2), a SEPARATE structure from the scalar mass-modes; photon spin-1 = two-singleton composite.

charge.how: hypercharge Y/2 = T_3R + (B-L)/2; the six SM hypercharges from the substrate.

color.how: color = the N_c=3 ODD off-diagonal Peirce directions; SU(3) h^v = N_c; odd N_c -> half-integer pi in the strong sector.

alpha.how: alpha_em = 1/137 = 1/N_max, N_max = N_c^3*n_C + rank; the boundary conformal scale.

theta_qcd.how: theta_QCD = 0 exactly (a symmetry-zero); strong CP resolved.

alpha_s.how: alpha_s RUNS (scale-dependent) -> not a single forced number; PROJECTS from the 3 strata; awaits #418.

weinberg.how: sin^2(theta_W) runs; identified form exists; gauge-couplings project from the 3 strata, gated on #418.

pair_production.how: LIGHT -> MATTER: a two-singleton composite (photon, spin-1) decomposing into single-singleton constituents (matter, spin-1/2); emission at the boundary in the COMMITTED regime (SWPP).

beta_decay.how: n -> p + e + nu_bar: trajectory relaxation from neutron (first excitation) to proton (ground state) + ~0.3% radiative photon; weak-only (below pion threshold).

confinement.how: thermal: colored states form HOT (deconfined, no free colored ground state); cooling FORCES them into singlets = confinement; proton = cold survivor.

commitment.how: SWPP: heat/light ABSORBED at boundary -> COMMITS by slotting a nu-axis ground state -> EMITTED at boundary (light if spread, matter if concentrated). bulk stores the committed count, edge emits.

commitment_to_cell_map.how: the map from a nu-axis stratum to a GF(128) Reed-Solomon cell. VERTEX (tau) case is a rigorous handle (4195): vertex=nu=0=trivial rep -> additive identity 0 = Frobenius FIXED POINT; commitment advances by the code's defining symmetry (Frobenius phi:x->x^2, PERIOD g) -> side = g, forbidding 127 (mult period, 0 off the circle) and 128 (field size). g=7 PRIME -> Frobenius orbits only size 1 or g, so the cell alphabet is RIGIDLY two-regime: two fixed points {0,1} + a sea of full-g-orbits (4196). DEPOSIT-LOCUS map (Casey's commitment picture, 4198/4199): three distinct loci for three distinct reps (Lyra F140) -- tau deposits at the VERTEX (point, GF(128) additive identity 0 = Frobenius fixed point, FORCED 4195), electron deposits on the SPECTRAL STRIP (continuum running-scale, self-dual axis nu=5/2; CORRECTS the earlier e<->1 discrete-fixed-point candidate which was a Cal #27 over-reach), muon deposits on the S^4 SPHERE (compact Shilov boundary, vol 8pi^2/3). DYNAMICS (4198): commitment = absorb->commit->emit on a Frobenius orbit; deposit point = beta^(2^k), the 'next integer' k = next Frobenius power; orbit has g points, closes after the Frobenius period g; Cal #374 PASS -- next-integer pre-selection is FORCED (k = iteration of the RS defining symmetry x->x^2), deterministic, no hidden parameter. PI-CHARACTER from LOCUS GEOMETRY: pi-ful <=> compact-sphere deposit (muon S^4 carries pi); pi-free <=> point (tau) or strip (electron). M_nu: neutrino loci #418-gated (continuum, Lyra's Bergman lane). Discrete (GF(128)) tau-side + continuum (strip/sphere) electron+muon-side joined by F139/F141 bridge; this map is the M_nu machinery gating PMNS + neutrino masses.
